items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-48907 Joomla Extension - joomlacontenteditornet - Remote Code Execution
id: 556fe011-35dc-5c3c-92dd-390af668f7bb
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-48907 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-48907
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/06
modified: 2026/06/06
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-48907 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-06T14:10:35Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-48907", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-48907 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:2739354; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Twig: Possible sandbox bypass when using a source policy
items.description:
# Description

When using the sandbox with a `SourcePolicyInterface`, Twig does not always apply the sandbox restriction that forbids non-`Closure` callbacks for callback-accepting filters.

The issue affects the `sort`, `filter`, `map`, and `reduce` filters.

In the affected versions, the runtime check that rejects non-`Closure` callbacks in sandbox mode does not use the current template `Source`. As a result, when the sandbox is enabled through a source policy instead of being enabled globally, Twig can incorrectly treat the current execution as non-sandboxed for these callback checks.

This can allow user-controlled templates to pass arbitrary PHP callables to callback-accepting filters even though the template is being sandboxed through a source policy.

The issue happens when all these conditions are met:

- The sandbox is not enabled globally;
- A `SourcePolicyInterface` enables the sandbox for the rendered template;
- The template uses one of the `sort`, `filter`, `map`, or `red
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 7.7/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-4035 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-4035
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/06
modified: 2026/06/06
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-4035 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-06T14:10:35Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-4035", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-4035 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7277444; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.apex_ai_summary: [P4] CVE / Vulnerability - low severity (Risk 2.2/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. Actor attribution: Vulnerability Exploitation Cluster. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: Cisco warns of unpatched SD-WAN zero-day exploited in attacks
items.description: On Thursday, Cisco warned of a high-severity, unpatched zero-day in the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (tracked as CVE-2026-20245) actively exploited in attacks enabling root privilege escalation. [...]
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] Threat Intel advisory. Risk score 9.0/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
